target.name: Lightmatter
target.founder: Nicholas Harris, Darius Bunandar, Thomas Graham

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $420M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
Lightmatter is a Series C chip-design company in the Semiconductors sector that has raised $420M+ to date. The valuation analysis considers a peer universe of 8 private companies and 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), though public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue.

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $714M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B Series B), and DEEPX ($529M Series C)—produces a median of $1.9B, with a range of $529M to $5.0B. The peer set spans adjacent stages because the sample is constrained; DEEPX at $529M offers a direct Series C comparable.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $607M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.3B. The $1.1B spread reflects material uncertainty driven by stage placement within peers and the wide dispersion of valuations at comparable funding levels. Key unknowns include revenue run-rate, capital efficiency relative to peers, and technology differentiation—none disclosed in available data. The peer-median method carries the most weight given direct stage alignment with DEEPX, though the presence of significantly higher-valued Series D peers (Tenstorrent, SambaNova) signals upside risk if Lightmatter's progress justifies elevation to that cohort.
